← All worlds/World 14
World 14 · 2 states

Burnout

Films for the person who kept going long after the tank was empty — the particular silence of having given too much for too long.

Choose an emotion
Empty AchievementCompassion Fatigue
On Empty Achievement

You reached it. The thing you told yourself would make everything make sense. You stood there and waited for the feeling and the feeling did not come. This is the particular cruelty of high-achieving emptiness — it has no obvious cause, no legible reason, no one to blame. You did everything right. You built the life. And you are standing in it wondering what went wrong. These films take that experience seriously. They do not offer solutions. They offer recognition, which is the rarer and more useful gift.

Empty Achievement

Films for the summit that felt like nothing.

🏆 FIPRESCI Prize Cannes

The Social Network

David Fincher · 2010

Magnolia

CS8.4

Lost in America

Albert Brooks · 1985

CS9.1
1999

Fight Club

David Fincher

View Film →

An insomniac office worker and a charismatic soap salesman form an underground fight club that evolves into something far darker — a film about the violence that follows emptiness.

CS8.8
1999

American Beauty

Sam Mendes

View Film →

A middle-aged suburban man in crisis falls obsessively for his daughter's friend while his wife and daughter pursue their own private desperation.

CS8.4
2009

Up in the Air

Jason Reitman

View Film →

A corporate downsizer who lives out of a suitcase and prides himself on having no attachments begins to question his perfectly engineered emptiness.

CS7.8
2017

Downsizing

Alexander Payne

View Film →

A middle-American man agrees to be miniaturized for environmental and economic reasons, and finds that a smaller life does not resolve the questions that tormented his full-sized one.

CS9.0
2016

Paterson

Jim Jarmusch

View Film →

A bus driver in Paterson, New Jersey spends a quiet week driving his route, writing poetry in a notebook, and living a life of ordinary, attentive dignity.

CS8.5
2002

About Schmidt

Alexander Payne

View Film →

A recently retired actuary discovers, with mounting horror, that his careful, productive life has added up to almost nothing — and tries, too late, to find what he missed.

CS9.1
2018

Burning

Lee Chang-dong

View Film →

A young deliveryman becomes obsessively fixated on a wealthy, mysterious man who claims to burn greenhouses for pleasure — a film about class, desire, and the violence of invisibility.

CS9.0
2010

The Social Network

David Fincher

View Film →

The founding of Facebook, told as a legal deposition — a film about a boy who wanted to belong so badly he built the world's largest social network and still ended up alone.

CS9.4
1999

Magnolia

Paul Thomas Anderson

View Film →

Nine interconnected lives in San Fernando Valley converge over one day and night — fathers and sons, ambition and regret, coincidence and consequence.

CS8.4
1985

Lost in America

Albert Brooks

View Film →

A successful advertising executive quits his job, convinces his wife to drop out of society, and discovers that dropping out is not as easy as dropping in.

For the slow rebuild after the wound.

Films don't just entertain. They quietly confirm you were seen — long before anyone in your real life knew how to.
